{"id":"project-LICHEN-k1wt","title":"senml: dec_text length cast could truncate on 16-bit targets","description":"File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 234, 239

The dec_text function casts length to usize:
```rust
let end = start + len as usize;  // line 234
Ok((s, adv + len as usize))      // line 239
```

While dec_head limits len to 65535, on theoretical 16-bit targets where usize is 16 bits, values >= 65536 would truncate. More importantly, even values up to 65535 would wrap when added to start if start is non-zero on a 16-bit target.

Rust's tier-1 targets all have 32+ bit usize, but the code is #![no_std] suggesting embedded use where 16-bit targets might be considered.

Actual risk on supported targets: None (min usize is 32-bit)
Theoretical risk: Wraparound arithmetic on hypothetical 16-bit targets

Impact: Theoretical only; defensive programming issue.

Note: This is primarily a documentation issue - the crate should document minimum target requirements (32-bit usize).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:41:24.403062Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:41:24.403092Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-k1wt","title":"senml: dec_text length cast could truncate on 16-bit targets","description":"File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 234, 239

The dec_text function casts length to usize:
```rust
let end = start + len as usize;  // line 234
Ok((s, adv + len as usize))      // line 239
```

While dec_head limits len to 65535, on theoretical 16-bit targets where usize is 16 bits, values >= 65536 would truncate. More importantly, even values up to 65535 would wrap when added to start if start is non-zero on a 16-bit target.

Rust's tier-1 targets all have 32+ bit usize, but the code is #![no_std] suggesting embedded use where 16-bit targets might be considered.

Actual risk on supported targets: None (min usize is 32-bit)
Theoretical risk: Wraparound arithmetic on hypothetical 16-bit targets

Impact: Theoretical only; defensive programming issue.

new_value: {"notes":"Commit: 46cfaa738 - dec_text already uses usize::try_from + checked_add for len/offsets (lines 269-278). Updated comment to reference bead and 16-bit safety. Theoretical issue resolved via defensive code."}
defensive checked arithmetic already present; docs updated (commit 46cfaa738)
